title: olearia odorata
intro: Olearia odorata, also known as Sweet Daisy or Pineapple Jasmine, is a flowering shrub native to Australia and Southeast Asia, characterized by its fragrant white or pink flowers and grayish-green leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Olearia odorata include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation and fever, and support respiratory health. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as infusions, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be used to make teas, salves, and other remedies to harness its medicinal properties.
